questions.question: What is the purpose of a 2:1 multiplexer?
options: A) To select one of two input signals
B) To select one of four input signals
C) To select one of three input signals
D) To select one of six input signals
questions.explanation: A 2:1 multiplexer is used to select one of two input signals.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a 4:1 multiplexer?
options: A) To select one of four input signals
B) To select one of two input signals
C) To select one of three input signals
D) To select one of six input signals
questions.explanation: A 4:1 multiplexer is used to select one of four input signals.

questions.question: How many bits are required to implement a 2:1 multiplexer?
options: A) 1 bit
B) 2 bits
C) 3 bits
D) 4 bits
questions.explanation: A 2:1 multiplexer requires 2 bits to select the input signal.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a 3:1 multiplexer?
options: A) To select one of three input signals
B) To select one of two input signals
C) To select one of four input signals
D) To select one of six input signals
questions.explanation: A 3:1 multiplexer is used to select one of three input signals.

questions.question: How many input signals can be combined using a 4:2 multiplexer?
options: A) 2 input signals
B) 3 input signals
C) 4 input signals
D) 5 input signals
questions.explanation: A 4:2 multiplexer can combine 4 input signals.
